sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Cardano Builder DAO treasury withdrawal to continue and expand a smart contract-governed, KPI-driven funding mechanism for Cardano builders
Recipient: Cardano Builder DAO, with funds received and administered initially by an independent dRep DAO council/multisig

Deliverables: Allocate capital to builders expected to improve Cardano ecosystem KPIs, Require funded projects to track monthly active users, monthly on-chain transactions, and TVL, Use public reporting and increasingly on-chain KPI verification, Disburse funds after milestone completion is validated by the administrator, Return unused or unallocated DAO treasury funds to the Cardano Treasury
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 20,000,000 ADA for the Cardano Builder DAO funding mechanism.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The Cardano Builder DAO is described as a smart contract-governed funding mechanism intended to allocate capital to builders that advance monthly active users, monthly on-chain transactions, and TVL.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The DAO has already distributed 11.1 million ADA across 34 proposals in two funding rounds.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): Round 1 allegedly included 38 voting members, 27 requesting members, 20 funded companies, an 83% governance participation rate, and 5,541,335 ADA distributed through a smart contract-governed process.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): Round 2 allegedly added 18 new members, funded 14 companies, and reached an 88% member participation rate after review, debate, temperature checks, and final voting.
Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The DAO built Version 1 and Version 2 of a KPI dashboard, with Version 2 moving toward KPI tracking tied to live on-chain Cardano data.
missing: Independent evidence for: The DAO has already distributed 11.1 million ADA across 34 proposals in two funding rounds.

Independent evidence for: Round 1 allegedly included 38 voting members, 27 requesting members, 20 funded companies, an 83% governance participation rate, and 5,541,335 ADA distributed through a smart contract-governed process.
Independent evidence for: Round 2 allegedly added 18 new members, funded 14 companies, and reached an 88% member participation rate after review, debate, temperature checks, and final voting.
Independent evidence for: The DAO built Version 1 and Version 2 of a KPI dashboard, with Version 2 moving toward KPI tracking tied to live on-chain Cardano data.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unknown (stale snapshot)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 20,000,000 ADA for the Cardano Builder DAO funding mechanism." and clears the evidence gates.
Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The DAO has already distributed 11.1 million ADA across 34 proposals in two funding rounds." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
